items.title: Police rearrest teen Mercedes driver for murder probe after fatal Simpang Renggam crash
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 5 — The teenage driver previously remanded in connection with reckless and dangerous driving that resulted in the deaths of four family members in Simpang Renggam near Kluang has been rearrested for a murder probe. Kluang district police chief ACP Bahrin Mohd Noh said the 19-year-old was initially remanded for two days beginning June 2 before the remand was extended by one day for investigations under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987. Bahrin had earlier said that the June 1 crash at Kilometre 27, Jalan Renggam-Simpang Renggam resulted in the deaths of four members of a family travelling in a Toyota Vios, including a 10-year-old girl, who were confirmed dead at the scene.

items.title: Malaysia needs clear policy and regulatory direction to maintain investor confidence: Fadillah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia needs clear policy and regulatory direction to maintain investor confidence and support the country's energy transition agenda, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said on Thursday. Fadillah said this during a Ministerial Session on "Powering Malaysia's Next Growth Chapter: Energy, Economy and Digital In Sync" at the Energy Transition Conference 2026 (ETCon26) here. Fadillah, who is also Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister, said his ministry would collaborate with other ministries to strengthen energy infrastructure as a strategic national enabler, moving beyond simply providing energy to meet market demand.

items.title: Leverage energy transition as a catalyst for economic restructuring: Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the energy transition agenda should not be viewed solely through the lens of decarbonisation as integration of energy, digitalisation and economic growth is crucial to strengthening Malaysia's resilience and long-term competitiveness. "We must look beyond treating energy and digitalisation as separate segments. Akmal Nasrullah said Malaysia must place greater emphasis on productivity and value creation as it pursues more integrated economic growth, as energy, digitalisation and economic complexity are closely interconnected.

items.title: Sentoria to be suspended, delisted over regularisation failure
items.summary: In a filing with Bursa Malaysia on Thursday, the diversified property developer and leisure operator said it would be removed from the Official List of Bursa Securities on June 16, 2026, unless it submits an appeal against the delisting by June 11, 2026. "In the event the company submits an appeal to Bursa Securities within the appeal timeframe, the delisting of the company's securities from the Official List of Bursa Securities on June 16, 2026, will be deferred pending a decision on the appeal. "The securities will be withdrawn from Bursa Depository by the company upon their delisting from the Official List of Bursa Securities," it said.

items.title: Australian far-right does U-turn on seizing foreigners' homes
items.summary: SYDNEY: A top representative of Australia's far-right One Nation party has clarified the party will not be kicking foreign permanent residents out of their homes if it takes power. An opinion poll last week showed One Nation, for decades a fringe group led by provocateur Pauline Hanson, had become the country's most popular party. Speaking on Sky News Australia on Thursday evening, One Nation lawmaker Barnaby Joyce said that if it won power the party would force foreign permanent residents to sell their homes or have their property seized by the state.

items.title: Japan May foreign reserves fall by US$77bil after large interventions
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan's foreign reserves fell by US$77 billion in May, government data showed on Friday, as Tokyo resumed massive interventions to stem the yen's slide, fuelling market speculation over how far it's willing to draw on its US$1.3 trillion war chest. The reserves stood at US$1.306 trillion at the end of May, down from US$1.383 trillion a month earlier, according to the Ministry of Finance (MOF) data. The data on Japan's foreign reserves, the world's second largest after China, came a week after separate MOF figures showed that Japan spent 11.7 trillion yen (US$73.13 billion) since late April to support the yen in what was the largest-ever intervention round in a month.

items.title: Japan plans to rebuild ageing nuclear plants to boost power supply
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan plans to rebuild between two and five ageing nuclear reactors by the 2040s and as many as 11 to 14 by the 2050s as it aims to secure stable power supply, public broadcaster NHK reported, citing a draft policy to be presented on Friday. Japan shut its 54 operating nuclear reactors after the 2011 Fukushima nuclear disaster, due to public fear about the industry's safety standards. Tokyo last year revised its basic energy policy to maximise the use of nuclear power.

items.title: Immigration ready for 10‑year Malaysian passports at RM350, over‑60s pay RM175, disabled free
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — The Malaysian Immigration Department (JIM) is ready to implement changes relating to passport validity of up to 10 years, as provided under the Fees (Passports and Visas) (Amendment) Order 2026 published in the Government Gazette Wednesday. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the implementation date would be announced by the government soon. The Fees (Passport and Visa) (Amendment) Order 2026, published in the Government Gazette yesterday, stipulates that the new Malaysian passport with a validity period of up to 10 years, will be issued at RM350, with implementation scheduled for this year.

items.title: Canada unveils national AI strategy
items.summary: OTTAWA: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Thursday launched the country's new national artificial intelligence (AI) strategy, reported Xinhua. It also aims to increase AI adoption from just over 12 per cent to 60 per cent by 2034, said the release, adding that a national AI literacy initiative will also be established to offer entry-level AI training for all Canadians. Canada currently has three national AI institutes: the Vector Institute in Toronto, Ontario; Mila - Quebec Artificial Intelligence Institute in Montreal, Quebec; and the Alberta Machine Intelligence Institute in Edmonton, Alberta.

items.title: Ex-SB officer avoids jail, fined RM10,000 over bribe from IS suspect's father
items.summary: Kuala Terengganu Sessions Court judge Mohd Azhar Othman ordered former assistant superintendent Mohd Nazli Mohamed, 44, to serve nine months in jail if he fails to pay the fine. Azhar said he did not impose a jail sentence as Mohd Nazli's wife is due to give birth to their third child this month. "The accused took advantage of Zaid's desperation to bring home his son and family.

items.title: 'Pay back or face court': No compromise for deliberate Mara loan defaulters, says chairman
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) said that it will not compromise with borrowers who deliberately default on their loans and is prepared to take them to court to safeguard public funds. Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said funds disbursed are public trust funds, not personal money, making it the borrowers' responsibility to repay them. Asyraf Wajdi said the strict approach was part of the Mara Transformation Programme currently being implemented.

items.title: DAP delays national conference to Aug as Johor and Negeri Sembilan dissolutions trigger election battle
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — The 2026 DAP National Conference, originally scheduled for July 12, has been postponed to August 16, party secretary-general Anthony Loke said. Loke said the selection of candidates to represent DAP in the two state elections would be determined by the party’s Candidate Selection Committee. Meanwhile, Loke, who is also the Seremban MP and former Chennah assemblyman, said DAP would campaign vigorously in the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state polls to help secure PH victories in both states.

items.title: Security alert disrupts Monash University Malaysia as police investigate bomb threat
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — Monash University Malaysia said today that normal operations have resumed at its Sunway City campus after police cleared a bomb threat reported last night. The university said it had responded to a security alert involving a possible bomb threat on campus and carried out a comprehensive security check with authorities. The university said the campus reopened today after police confirmed there was no longer any threat.

items.title: Mortgage release process is long-standing practice, not new policy
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Public Sector Housing Financing Board (LPPSA) has clarified that the process of discharging a charge or transferring ownership rights after a housing loan has been fully settled is a long-established practice and not a new policy, as perceived by some parties. "The process of discharging a charge or transferring ownership rights after financing has been fully settled has long been practised as part of the procedures following the completion of financing. "The recommendation to appoint a lawyer is aimed at protecting borrowers' interests and ensuring that the discharge of charge process is completed properly.

items.title: Explain restrictions on The Coverage Media's FB page, Teo tells MCMC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching has called on the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) to provide an immediate explanation over the restriction imposed on The Coverage Media's Facebook page. "I have always maintained a policy of non-interference regarding MCMC's independent enforcement operations. "However, I strongly urge MCMC to provide an immediate explanation for the restriction placed on The Coverage Media's Facebook page," she said in a Facebook post.

items.title: Wall St ends higher; Dow claims record closing high
items.summary: NEW YORK: Wall Street advanced on Thursday as progress toward ending the Iran war buoyed investor sentiment, while disappointing results from Broadcom led a chip selloff that held the Nasdaq's gains in check. The blue-chip Dow surged, hitting a record closing high with a boost from healthcare and financial stocks. Chipmaker Broadcom missed revenue expectations, sending its shares tumbling and casting a pall over the AI frenzy, which has sent chip stocks soaring so far this year.

items.title: PH to launch Johor election machinery tomorrow
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan (PH) will launch its election machinery for the Johor polls in Batu Pahat tomorrow night. "Johor people are ready. Johor is not afraid to face an election.

items.title: US imposes sanctions on Cuban president, Castro family members
items.summary: HAVANA: The United States announced new sanctions Thursday on Cuba's president and some of his immediate family, alongside members of the Castro family, in Washington's latest ramping up of pressure on its communist-led neighbor. While Cuba has been under a US trade embargo since 1962, President Donald Trump has drastically ramped up pressure in recent months, cutting off its fuel supplies and threatening to take over the island. Washington claims that Cuba's communist regime threatens the security of Americans.

items.title: DAP to defend all seats in Johor and Negri, postpones special congress
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DAP will defend all its seats in Johor and Negri Sembilan and is seeking to contest additional seats in Johor in the upcoming state elections, says secretary-general Anthony Loke. Loke also announced that the DAP Central Executive Committee (CEC) had decided to postpone the party's special congress from July 12 to Aug 16. He said the party's candidate selection committee for the state elections would comprise himself, DAP chairman Gobind Singh Deo, deputy chairman Nga Kor Ming, DAP Wanita chief Teo Nie Ching and parliamentary leader Chong Chieng Jen.

items.title: Correcting without criticising
items.summary: It also requires addressing poor performance, inappropriate behaviour, and mistakes when they occur. Many leaders find reprimanding staff uncomfortable because they worry about damaging relationships, lowering morale, or creating conflict. Effective leaders understand that reprimanding staff is sometimes necessary, but it must be done professionally and without becoming personal.

items.title: Corporate earnings outlook turns cautious, weak quarter raises red flags [WATCH]
items.summary: RHB Research said four key sectors - banks, plantations, property and rubber products - missed expectations during the quarter, with no earnings beats recorded. In banking, Malayan Banking Bhd's first-quarter net profit fell 4.2 per cent to RM2.48 billion, while CIMB Group Holdings Bhd posted a slight decline in net profit to RM1.92 billion. Plantation heavyweight SD Guthrie Bhd also recorded a marginal earnings decline as weaker upstream performance weighed on results.

items.title: Elon Musk 'trying to whip up division' over UK student's murder: PM
items.summary: LONDON: UK leader Keir Starmer accused US tech tycoon Elon Musk on Thursday of "trying to whip up division" in Britain following anger over the police handling of the murder of a white student by a Sikh man. Digwa, 23, lied and told police he was the victim and that Nowak had racially insulted him. Far-right figures have claimed the murder is evidence that police forces in Britain treat white people and ethnic minorities differently – an allegation Starmer's Labour government and police chiefs vehemently deny.

items.title: Pas eyes broader PN+ coalition with Umno among potential partners [WATCH]
items.summary: Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said the proposal was among the recommendations made during the party's recent syura council meeting. Tuan Ibrahim, who is also PN deputy chairman, said the proposal was among several suggestions aimed at strengthening and improving the coalition. Tuan Ibrahim said PN+ would include parties seeking to join the coalition.

items.title: Trump foe Bolton to plead guilty to retaining classified info
items.summary: WASHINGTON: John Bolton, who served as Donald Trump's national security advisor before becoming an outspoken critic of the US president, plans to plead guilty to one count of retaining classified documents, US media reported on Thursday. Bolton, one of several Trump foes to be hit with criminal charges since the Republican president returned to the White House, was indicted in October on 18 counts of transmitting and retaining top secret national defence information. CNN said Bolton will plead guilty to one felony count of illegal retention of sensitive national security information, which carries a sentence of up to 60 months in prison.

items.title: Malaysia must strengthen trade checks to counter US tariff threat [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia must strengthen enforcement and supply chain checks amid scrutiny from the United States over forced labour and excess capacity concerns, said legal experts. LACK OF VERIFICATION Senior advocate and international commercial arbitrator GK Ganesan Kasinathan said it would be inaccurate to suggest that Malaysia lacks the legal authority to act. STRONGER ENFORCEMENT NEEDED Ganesan said Malaysia should use the period before any final US tariff decision to demonstrate its commitment to enforcement.

items.title: Is ‘economy rice’ still the economical choice? How mixed rice/nasi campur sellers are navigating current challenges
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 5 — The word “economy” in economy rice once had a straightforward meaning. Prices have risen, margins have narrowed and competition has intensified, transforming economy rice from one of Malaysia’s cheapest meals into a category increasingly defined by value, variety and quality. Once associated primarily with affordability, many customers today judge outlets based on the variety of dishes available, food quality, cleanliness and overall dining experience.

items.title: Queen visits Chow Kit school for underprivileged children
items.summary: HER Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, the Queen of Malaysia, visited Sekolah Bimbingan Jalinan Kasih for underprivileged children yesterday as part celebrations marking the king's birthday. According to a post on the Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar Facebook page, the queen was received by the prime minister's wife Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek and the Prime Minister's Department senior deputy secretary-general Datuk Abd Shukor Mahmood. Sekolah Bimbingan Jalinan Kasih currently provides formal education to 158 marginalised and orphaned children in the Chow Kit area.

items.title: Hundreds in Libya protest against migrants, refugees
items.summary: TRIPOLI: Hundreds of Libyans gathered outside the UN refugee agency headquarters in Tripoli on Thursday to protest against irregular migrants and refugees whom they say should leave Libya. They accused the UN agency of seeking to settle irregular migrants in the North African country. The UN mission in Libya in a statement said it "refutes allegations of migrant resettlement to Libya."

items.title: Undocumented siblings get proper education chance
items.summary: THE Education Ministry and National Registration Department (NRD) are leading an inter-agency effort to help six undocumented siblings who have never attended school. The intervention follows Monday's report by 'Harian Metro' on the family's situation. On Wednesday, officials from the ministry, NRD, Selangor Zakat Board and Hulu Langat District Education Office, as well as representatives from the Hulu Langat parliamentary office, visited the family's home to assess their immediate welfare and educational needs.

items.title: TNG Digital non-payments business surpasses payments revenue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: TNG Digital Sdn Bhd's non-payments business has overtaken the payments segment in revenue. TNG Digital said this underscores its transformation from a digital payments provider into a broader financial and lifestyle ecosystem. Chief executive officer Alan Ni said the non-payments business accounted for 51 per cent of total revenue in 2025.

items.title: WHO: Unsafe food kills 1.5m people worldwide yearly
items.summary: FOOD contaminated with harmful bacteria, viruses, parasites or chemicals kills 1.5 million people worldwide each year, with young children worst hit, the World Health Organisation (WHO) warned yesterday. "Food safety is not an abstract issue — it touches every meal, every family, every day," said WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. Although illnesses from ingesting dangerous food have declined overall since 2000, massive regional inequalities remain.

items.title: Implementation date for 10-year passport to be announced soon by govt: Immigration DG
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department is ready to implement changes relating to passport validity of up to 10 years, as provided under the Fees (Passports and Visas) (Amendment) Order 2026 published in the Government Gazette. Immigration director‑general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the implementation date would be announced by the government soon. Zakaria said the department is currently issuing passports with a five‑year validity period and would continue to do so, with the public to be given a choice once the new validity period is implemented.

items.title: Restaurant worker under probe over alleged cat abuse in viral video
items.summary: IPOH: Police have opened an investigation paper into a case involving a man who allegedly abused a cat at a restaurant in Taman Melor, Teluk Intan. A 1‑minute 32‑second video showing the man, believed to be a restaurant worker, placing a cat into a sack recently went viral on social media. "Police have also received a report lodged by the restaurant owner regarding the viral video.

items.title: Tiny Montenegro vies to be EU's next member but hurdles remain
items.summary: WHEN Air Montenegro's passenger planes took to the skies earlier this year, they were branded with a new slogan: "28 by 28 - The Next EU Member." All 27 current EU states would have to ratify Montenegrin membership before it could join. "Montenegro is the most advanced candidate country in the accession negotiations," EU Enlargement Commissioner Marta Kos told Reuters.

items.title: Anwar vows steady backing for high-quality investments
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim yesterday met AT&S chief executive officer Dr Michael Mertin to discuss the latest developments regarding the company's operations in Kulim, Kedah. In a Facebook post, Anwar said the company currently employs around 2,700 workers in Kulim. He added that Malaysia is one of AT&S's major production hubs, alongside Austria, China and India, reflecting global investors' continued confidence in the country's capabilities and competitiveness.

items.title: Countdown to election: Negeri Sembilan MB announces formal dissolution of state assembly
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 4 — The Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly, which has 36 seats, will be officially dissolved tomorrow to pave the way for the upcoming State Election (PRN). Aminuddin said the official notice of the State Legislative Assembly’s dissolution will be submitted to the Election Commission (EC) soon for the electoral body to determine key dates for the upcoming State Election (PRN). The 15th State Election in 2023 saw Pakatan Harapan and Barisan Nasional form the Negeri Sembilan government after securing a combined 31 of the 36 state seats.

items.title: Five passengers injured as Kelana Jaya train halts unexpectedly near Dang Wangi station (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Five passengers on the Kelana Jaya Line were injured when the train they were on came to an abrupt stop after its automatic safety system was activated this evening. In a statement, Rapid KL said the incident involved train number 62 near Dang Wangi Station at 6.07 pm. The statement said the incident occurred when the train’s automatic safety system was triggered when the train was departing the station, causing it to stop immediately as an operational control measure.

items.title: Election season beckons: Negri Sembilan assembly expected to be next to be dissolved by tomorrow
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 4 — The Negri Sembilan state assembly is expected to be dissolved tomorrow, clearing the way for snap elections as the state’s political impasse continues. Sources told the newspaper that Pakatan Harapan leaders have tentatively agreed on their seat allocations:PKR is expected to contest 16 seats, DAP 11, and Amanah nine in the 36‑seat assembly. Senior state leaders, including DAP’s Anthony Loke and Amanah’s Datuk MK Ibrahim Abd Rahman, are expected to join Aminuddin at Friday’s announcement.

items.title: Bersatu Endau state rep announces exit from party, rejoins Umno
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 4 — Endau assemblyman Alwiyah Talib today announced that she has left Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) and rejoined Umno. Alwiyah, who is also the Mersing Bersatu division chief, said the decision was made after considering various factors for the benefit and future of the constituency she represents. In the 2022 Johor state election, Alwiyah, representing Perikatan Nasional (PN), retained the Endau state seat with a majority of 3,041 votes in a five-cornered contest.

items.title: Lorry driver fined RM8,000, jailed one day for obscene gesture and dangerous driving in Penang
items.summary: BALIK PULAU, June 4 — A lorry driver was fined RM8,000, jailed for one day and disqualified from holding a driving licence for five years by the Magistrate’s Court here today after pleading guilty to two charges of making an obscene gesture and dangerous driving last Thursday. Magistrate Chia Huey Ting imposed the sentence on Tan Chee Gang, 37, and ordered him to serve six months’ imprisonment if he failed to pay the fine. Chia imposed a RM3,000 fine for the first offence and one day’s imprisonment, a RM5,000 fine, endorsement of the driving licence and a five-year driving disqualification for the second offence.

items.title: Murder suspect in decomposed body case in Melaka remanded for further seven days
items.summary: MELAKA, June 4 — The suspect in a murder case involving the discovery of a decomposed body wrapped in a mattress with his hands tied in a shophouse in the Pokok Mangga area here has been remanded for another seven days starting today. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the remand period of the suspect, a 40-year-old local man, has been extended to assist in the investigation into the case. Previously, the media reported that a decomposed body with both hands tied, wrapped in a mattress, was found in a shophouse in Pokok Mangga on May 28.

items.title: Zahid: Agricultural TVET strengthened to support agro-food sector and national food security
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 4 — The National TVET Council (MTVET) Meeting No. 2/2026 today focused on strengthening agricultural Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) as a strategic agenda to support the development of the agro-food sector and enhance national food security. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, in a statement, said the meeting, which he chaired, also reviewed several strategic initiatives to reinforce the country’s TVET ecosystem, particularly in sectors that are key drivers of economic growth and national development.

items.title: Rohingya man pleads not guilty to dangerous driving charge linked to fatal crash in Marang
items.summary: MARANG, June 4 — A Rohingya man pleaded not guilty at the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of dangerous driving causing the death of a motorcyclist on Jalan Kuala Terengganu-Kuantan, near Rusila here, last week. Shah Dullah Abul Hashim, 20, made the plea after the charge was read out to him before Magistrate Nur Athirah Hashim. The act was allegedly committed at Kilometre 12.5 of Jalan Kuala Terengganu-Kuantan near Kampung Rusila here at 6 pm on May 27.

items.title: Malaysia poised to deepen food security, innovation ties with Chile after Asean business summit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Chile is looking to deepen cooperation with Malaysia in food security, innovation and sustainable development, following the Chile-Asean Business Summit 2026 held recently in Bangkok, Thailand. Malaysia remains an important market for Chilean products. The Chile-Asean Business Summit 2026 featured 28 Chilean companies representing sectors including seafood, fresh fruit, meat products, nuts and dried fruits, forestry products, agribusiness, aquaculture services and food innovation.

items.title: Queen visits Chow Kit school for vulnerable children and presents RM30,000 contribution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, today presented a RM30,000 cheque contribution to Sekolah Bimbingan Jalinan Kasih (SBJK) here. The donation was bestowed during Her Majesty's visit to the school as part of celebrations marking the official birthday of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, the King of Malaysia. Receiving Her Majesty on arrival were Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, the wife of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek and Education Ministry secretary-general Datuk Wan Hashim Wan Rahim.

items.title: Man with rape and robbery history claims trial with wife over abuse of her three-year-old daughter
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — A husband and wife pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of abusing a three-year-old girl, causing her physical injuries, last month. Nightclub bouncer Hamzi Amni Zamali, 40, and housewife Nurazwani Ahmad Rosli, 29, were jointly charged, as persons responsible for the child's care, with abusing the girl and causing physical injuries at a low-cost flat unit in Bandar Tasik Selatan here on May 23, 2026. However, should the court allow bail, she proposed bail of RM30,000 for each accused, with an additional condition requiring them to report to the nearest police station twice a month.

items.title: Delayed for a year, Penang’s revised water tariffs finally take effect on July 1
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 4 — Penang will implement new water tariff for domestic and non-domestic consumers starting July 1 after its implementation was postponed for 12 months. State Transport, Infrastructure and Digital Committee chairman Zairil Khir Johari said the average domestic water tariff rate for the first 35 cubic metres (m³) of water used per month has been set at RM0.93 per m³. He said the adjustment involved a maximum increase in water bills of RM2.55 per month for 82 per cent of domestic consumers or households who use 35 m³ of water or less per month, equivalent to about 8.5 sen per day.

items.title: ‘We cannot turn a blind eye’: Onn Hafiz defends RM66m health spending for Johor
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, June 4 — The Johor state government has spent more than RM66 million to strengthen the state's public health sector over the past four years of its administration. Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said this allocation was distributed to ensure the welfare and health of approximately four million people in the state continue to be preserved. “If following the old way, the state government would not need to channel more than RM66 million to the Johor State Health Department (JKNJ).

items.title: Melaka GH, Masjid Tanah, Umbai and Merlimau lets patients drive-through to get their meds
items.summary: MELAKA, June 4 — Melaka has introduced drive-through pharmacy services at four healthcare facilities, enabling patients to collect their medication more conveniently without having to queue at pharmacy counters, Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh said. He said the service is available at Melaka Hospital and the Masjid Tanah, Umbai and Merlimau Health Clinics as part of efforts to improve access to healthcare services. Implemented by the Melaka Health Department under its Value Added Services (VAS) programme, the facility allows patients to obtain their medication without leaving their vehicles.

items.title: Johari says USTR's excess capacity claim may stem from third party imports
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — The US Trade Representative’s (USTR) claim of excess capacity against Malaysia may have stemmed from imports from other countries, Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said today as Putrajaya insists Malaysian manufacturers are not overproducing to flood the American market. USTR had cited suspected forced-labour concerns and alleged excess capacity among Malaysian manufacturers as reasons for a proposed 10 per cent tariff on certain Malaysian exports. “It could be that we import products that come from third parties with excess capacity, so that’s why.” Johari maintained that Malaysian manufacturers were not producing beyond market demand in an attempt to flood the US market.

items.title: King tells MACC’s new chief to tackle graft without fear or favour
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has advised newly appointed Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman to carry out his duties with the highest level of integrity. According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar's Facebook page, His Majesty stressed that efforts to combat corruption must be pursued continuously, firmly and without fear or favour to safeguard integrity and uphold the rule of law. On April 25, the King consented to Abdul Halim's appointment as the new MACC chief commissioner, effective May 13.

items.title: New wild ginger species discovered in Lenggong, highlighting Perak’s biodiversity, says Saarani
items.summary: IPOH, June 4 — Researchers have discovered a new species of wild ginger, Alpinia lenggongensis, in Lenggong, a finding that further underscores Perak’s rich biodiversity and natural heritage, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said today. He said the species was discovered by researchers from Universiti Putra Malaysia and Universiti Malaysia Kelantan. “Lenggong has been a Unesco World Heritage Site since 2012 and was officially recognised as the Lenggong Unesco Global Geopark on April 27, 2026,” he said.

items.title: Govt taps military retirees to fill shortage of bus, lorry drivers and aviation technicians
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 4 — The government is turning to retired military personnel to help address labour shortages in the transport sector, including as lorry and bus drivers as well as skilled workers in the aviation maintenance industry, under a new collaboration between the Transport Ministry (MOT) and Defence Ministry (Mindef). At a joint press conference later with Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin, Loke said the initiative would also help address manpower shortages across various segments of the transport industry. We do not have enough bus and truck drivers,” he said.

items.title: Johor MCA rejects viral state election lineup, says candidate selection still under review
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, June 4 — Johor MCA today dismissed as fake a purported state election candidates’ list circulating on social media that names party president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong as the candidate for the Skudai seat. Johor MCA deputy chief Ling Tian Soon said the list is not an official party document and does not reflect any decision made by the party. He said Johor MCA has never issued any candidate list or document related to the upcoming state election.

items.title: Wisma Putra: All registered Malaysians in Kuwait safe after drone and missile strike on airport
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Malaysia's Foreign Ministry has confirmed that there have been no reports of Malaysians being affected by the drone and missile attack targeting Kuwait International Airport in the early hours of June 3. The ministry, through the Malaysian Embassy in Kuwait, is closely monitoring the situation following the attack and has confirmed that all registered Malaysian citizens in the country are safe. “Malaysian citizens whose travel plans have been affected by the disruption are advised to contact their respective airlines directly for alternative arrangements and flight rescheduling,” the ministry said in a statement on Thursday.

items.title: Malaysia, Japan can deepen ties in clean energy and high-tech sectors, says Fadillah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Malaysia's efforts to remain economically competitive while meeting its sustainability commitments responsibly and realistically will create significant opportunities for collaboration with Japan, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said. Fadillah, who is also the Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation, said both countries could further strengthen cooperation, particularly in renewable energy, hydrogen development, energy efficiency, green infrastructure and sustainable industrial solutions. “Malaysia sees tremendous potential in renewable energy, green technology, digital infrastructure, advanced manufacturing, data centres, smart logistics, artificial intelligence and cross-border connectivity, sectors where Japanese expertise, innovation and global experience can complement Malaysia's strengths and ambitions.

items.title: After missing hiker case, Saarani urges climbers to engage Orang Asli communities
items.summary: IPOH, June 4 — Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad today urged organisers to inform Tok Batin and Orang Asli communities in advance whenever hiking expeditions are conducted in forests inhabited by indigenous groups. Saarani said forest conservation and management should not be viewed solely as the responsibility of the Forestry Department, as Orang Asli communities have lived in and around these forests for generations and possess a deep understanding of the environment. Orang Asli are already serving as rangers, and we also have Orang Asli working as mountain guides,” he said.

items.title: After childbirth, back to class: Fadhlina insists pregnant students complete SPM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — Teenage girls involved in out-of-wedlock pregnancies will continue to receive support to ensure they return to school and sit for the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said. She said these students must be given every opportunity to continue their education and avoid dropping out of the school system. Fadhlina said that once a pregnancy is discovered, the teenager should be taken to the nearest health clinic or rural health centre to register for maternal healthcare and open a pregnancy health record or the "pink book”.

items.title: Zahid: BN to name Johor candidates a week before nomination day
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 4 — Barisan Nasional (BN) will finalise the list of candidates for the 56 Johor State Legislative Assembly seats in the near future before announcing them a week before nomination day, said its chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. In addition, he said BN would also feature a combination between existing office holders and new faces to colour the leadership talent in Johor. This time, new faces will also colour the faces of new leadership talents in Johor,” he said.

items.title: Thunderstorms and heavy rain this afternoon in parts of Selangor, Pahang, Terengganu, Johor and Negeri Sembilan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 4 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in five states until 3pm today. MetMalaysia in a statement today announced that the situation is occurring in Terengganu (Dungun and Kemaman), Pahang (Jerantut) and Selangor (Hulu Langat). Similar conditions are also expected to hit Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol and Tampin) and Johor (Segamat, Kluang, Mersing, Pontian, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru).
